meta.description: Public example deck. Drag this into the app to test the flow, then replace with your own cards.

cards.presentation: 62-year-old man with 45 minutes of crushing substernal chest pain, diaphoresis, and ST elevation in leads II, III, aVF.
cards.one_thing: ST elevation in inferior leads (II, III, aVF) = inferior STEMI from RCA -> emergent PCI within 90 minutes.
cards.educational_objective: Inferior STEMI is commonly caused by right coronary artery occlusion. Emergent PCI is preferred when available within 90 minutes of first medical contact.
cards.board_trigger: IF ST elevation in II, III, aVF -> inferior STEMI -> activate cath lab -> PCI within 90 minutes
cards.why_not_others: Unstable angina has no ST elevation and no biomarker rise. Pericarditis usually causes diffuse ST elevation rather than a contiguous coronary territory.
cards.explanation: The RCA supplies the inferior wall in most patients. Inferior STEMI should prompt emergent reperfusion and consideration of right-sided ECG leads because RV infarct can change nitrate management.
cards.quick_recall: Inferior STEMI: ST elevation II/III/aVF -> RCA -> PCI within 90 minutes.

cards.presentation: Trauma patient on mechanical ventilation develops sudden hypotension, absent left breath sounds, tracheal deviation to the right, and rising peak airway pressures.
cards.one_thing: Tension pneumothorax is a clinical diagnosis. Decompress immediately; do not wait for chest x-ray.
cards.educational_objective: Tension pneumothorax causes obstructive shock from air under pressure compressing the lung and mediastinum. Immediate needle decompression is life-saving and should be followed by chest tube placement.
cards.board_trigger: IF hypotension + unilateral absent breath sounds + tracheal deviation -> tension pneumothorax -> needle decompression now
cards.why_not_others: Hemothorax can cause absent breath sounds but classically has dullness to percussion and blood loss. Mainstem intubation causes unilateral breath sounds but does not usually cause obstructive shock with tracheal deviation.
cards.explanation: Air enters the pleural space under pressure, collapses the ipsilateral lung, shifts the mediastinum, and impairs venous return. Treat before imaging when unstable.
cards.quick_recall: Tension pneumothorax: hypotension + absent breath sounds + tracheal shift -> immediate decompression.

cards.presentation: 65-year-old with small cell lung cancer has sodium 122, low serum osmolality, euvolemic exam, urine sodium greater than 40, and urine osmolality greater than 100.
cards.one_thing: SIADH = euvolemic hypotonic hyponatremia with inappropriately concentrated urine. Start with fluid restriction.
cards.educational_objective: SIADH presents with hypotonic hyponatremia, euvolemia, urine sodium greater than 40 mEq/L, urine osmolality greater than 100 mOsm/kg, and normal thyroid/adrenal function.
cards.board_trigger: IF euvolemic hyponatremia + urine Na >40 + urine Osm >100 + malignancy -> SIADH -> fluid restriction
cards.why_not_others: Hypovolemic hyponatremia has volume depletion signs. Hypervolemic hyponatremia has edema or ascites. Hypothyroidism and adrenal insufficiency must be excluded.
cards.explanation: Excess ADH retains free water and dilutes serum sodium. Urine remains concentrated despite low serum osmolality. Correct sodium slowly to avoid osmotic demyelination.
cards.quick_recall: SIADH: euvolemic hypo-Na + urine Na >40 + urine Osm >100 -> fluid restrict.
